description: Phi uses 'thiku' at the small end of overall scale. It suits a little stone or a small person, and it also works for amounts and undertakings. 'hieru' (narrow) and 'teku' (short) select width and end-to-end extent.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip at the upper teeth for voiceless 'th' and let air pass into high-front unrounded 'i'. The back of the tongue then closes against the soft palate for 'k' and releases into rounded high-back 'u'. Stress falls on 'thi'.
sound_symbolism: High-front 'i' keeps the first syllable compact, and the closure before 'k' briefly gathers the word again. The result feels neatly contained, an easy memory hook for little size.
usage_notes: Scale comes from the scene. A small rabbit among larger rabbits is 'thiku' even when no measurement is given.

semantic_domains.physical: small overall size or amount relative to the immediate comparison
semantic_domains.spatial: limited extent considered as a whole rather than along one axis
